title: External MCP Integrations (Jira + Graphify)
summary: Module 09 integrates Jira and Graphify into Coodra with one shared pattern: wire the external system's own MCP server into the agent's config, and have Coodra provide fusion tools + skill recipes that compose it with the knowledge layer. Coodra builds no Jira REST client and no Graphify graph reader. Track 9A (Jira) = Direct: onboarding writes Atlassian's Rovo MCP server (mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp/authv2, per-user OAuth); Epic->Feature Pack, Story->Run, on-request write-back. Track 9B (Graphify) = Option C: onboarding writes Graphify's own stdio MCP server (python -m graphify.serve); the dead query_codebase_graph tool + graphify.ts reader are retired; a new seed_feature_packs_from_graph tool turns Leiden communities into draft Feature Packs; get_feature_pack gains a structure block. A shared 9-Core substrate (MCP-config writer, /settings/integrations page, coodra <x> enable/disable/status CLI) is built once. Supersedes the build-our-own Jira design in system-architecture.md section 22 and the original ADR-010 Graphify reader/importer.
supersedes: system-architecture.md section 22 — build-our-own Jira REST client + OAuth app + webhooks
Original ADR-010 / section 17 — Coodra-owned Graphify graph.json reader + community importer
